description: The orchid you see everywhere — and for good reason. Blooms last 2-3 months, re-blooms yearly with minimal effort. Not the diva people think.

care.water_frequency: Every 7-10 days: soak the bark, let it drain completely
care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light — east or north-facing window
care.light_also_ok: Can tolerate lower light, but may not re-bloom
care.humidity_action: Light misting in dry winters helps. Never let water pool in the crown — causes rot.
care.soil_types: Orchid bark mix (bark, sphagnum, perlite). Never use regular potting soil — roots need air!
care.repot_frequency: Every 2-3 years after flowering, when bark decomposes
care.fertilizer_type: Orchid-specific fertilizer (20-20-20) every 2 weeks, half strength
care.difficulty: Easy — despite its reputation, very forgiving if you follow two rules: no soggy roots, no direct sun
care.watering_guide: Soak the bark for 10-15 minutes, let all water drain out completely. Roots should go from silver to green after watering. Water again when roots turn silver.
care.light_guide: East-facing window is perfect. No direct afternoon sun — burns leaves. If leaves are dark green, it needs more light. Light green with slight yellow tinge = perfect.
care.tips: After all flowers drop: cut the spike above the second node from the base — often triggers a second bloom. Green roots = healthy, silver roots = thirsty. Use a clear pot to see root health.
care.toxicity_note: Completely non-toxic. Safe around kids and pets.
common_problems: Bud blast / buds falling off (moved the plant or temperature shock — pick a spot and leave it)
Wrinkled leaves (roots are dead or dehydrated — check root health)
Yellow lower leaf (normal aging if one at a time. Multiple = overwatering)
common_pests: Mealybugs (cottony white masses near leaf joints — isolate and treat with alcohol)
Scale (brown or tan bumps — scrape off, treat with neem)
Thrips (silvery streaks on flowers — remove affected flowers)
